channel.title: RISC-V on Vamsi Kiran | Firmware Engineer
channel.description: Recent content in RISC-V on Vamsi Kiran | Firmware Engineer

item.title: ZX-8080 MCU: Bare-Metal Peripheral Control
item.description:
🛠 Technology Stack

  • Architecture: RISC-V 32-bit (ZX-8080 Instruction Set)
  • Language: Bare-Metal C (Register-level)
  • Clock System: 80 MHz System Frequency
  • Key Concepts: Memory-Mapped I/O (MMIO), Clock Trees, SAR ADC Polling

The Project Overview

This project serves as a deep-dive sandbox for mastering fundamental Firmware Engineering. I implemented low-level drivers for a custom 32-bit RISC-V microcontroller to manage thermal monitoring and precision timing. By interfacing directly with hardware registers based on datasheet specifications, I bypassed abstraction layers to optimize performance and memory footprint.
